title: Worst Episode Ever

jokes.text: Mmm, good pancakes, Mom. Well, thank you, honey. They come in a squeeze bottle now.
jokes.setup: Child compliments mom's homemade pancakes
jokes.punchline: They're actually from a squeeze bottle - modern convenience replacing cooking
jokes.explanation: Simple domestic humor about modern convenience culture. Well-structured but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: [Flatulence Sound] Whoo! [Clears Throat] I better put on the silencer. [Silenced Blasts]
jokes.setup: Homer farts loudly at breakfast table
jokes.punchline: He has a 'silencer' device for his flatulence that makes muffled fart sounds
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of bathroom humor with creative visual gag. Classic Homer behavior with inventive twist.

jokes.text: How long has this baking soda been in here? I don't know. It came with the house.
jokes.setup: Marge questions age of baking soda in fridge
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals it's been there since they moved in - suggesting decades
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness. Economical setup-punchline with relatable domestic situation.

jokes.text: The absorbed odors of a million meals. Oh, so many flavors! All those tasty memories flooding back.
jokes.setup: Homer eating ancient baking soda
jokes.punchline: He's experiencing it like fine wine tasting - sophisticated appreciation of gross thing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer treating disgusting thing as gourmet experience. Well-executed character comedy with poetic language contrast.

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Dad's having an antacid trip. And I won 50 bucks.
jokes.setup: Homer hallucinating from baking soda consumption
jokes.punchline: Bart calls it an 'antacid trip' (pun on acid trip) and calmly notes his financial win
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun that works on multiple levels. Bart's casual attitude toward Homer's condition is perfect character work.

jokes.text: My doctor says I'm not supposed to go on sprees. What about jags? Jags are fine. Wonderful.
jokes.setup: Bart suggests going on a spending spree
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's medical restriction is oddly specific about 'sprees' vs 'jags'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse neurosis - absurdly specific medical advice. Good wordplay on synonymous terms.

jokes.text: Our wide variety of gum comes in both stick and ball. I'm not really about gum. But I like the whole chewing thing.
jokes.setup: Clerk describing gum selection
jokes.punchline: Bart's philosophical distinction between liking chewing but not gum specifically
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's weird logic. Clerk's presentation is also amusingly formal.

jokes.text: My mom doesn't believe in fabric softener. But she's not around!
jokes.setup: Boys spending money freely
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's rebellion is using fabric softener against his mother's beliefs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse character work - his idea of rebellion is hilariously tame and specific to his repressed household.

jokes.text: It is in perma-mint condition. If you spill soda on it, the drops fly off harmlessly onto lesser comics.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy selling expensive comic
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'permanent'/'mint' and absurd claim about liquid-repelling properties
jokes.explanation: Excellent Comic Book Guy dialogue - perfect pun with character-specific obsessive detail and pseudo-scientific nonsense.

jokes.text: Film reel labeled 'Alternate Ending: Luke's Father is Chewbacca'
jokes.setup: Reading valuable Star Wars memorabilia
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous alternate plot twist that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Absurd Star Wars parody that works because it's completely nonsensical

jokes.text: Don't do it, lady! That stuff's worth thousands! Yeah! He's ripping you off! Well, if this is valuable, then back to the leaky basement it goes!
jokes.setup: Boys try to help woman getting ripped off
jokes.punchline: Their honesty backfires - she takes the valuable items away instead of selling them
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal where doing the right thing backfires. Shows consequences of honest intervention.

jokes.text: Son, I know it hurts. I still remember my first lifetime banning.
jokes.setup: Bart getting banned from comic store
jokes.punchline: Homer treats lifetime banning as a normal rite of passage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - treating inappropriate experiences as normal. Sets up flashback nicely.

jokes.text: And you are banned for life from all of my performances and TV specials! But I can still see your movies, right? [Groans]
jokes.setup: Gallagher banning young Homer from performances
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent question reveals he doesn't understand Gallagher only does live shows
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Homer's obliviousness about Gallagher's career. Builds the flashback story well.

jokes.text: Hello. I'm Shaquille O'Neal.
jokes.setup: Homer on Bart's shoulders trying to get into comic store
jokes.punchline: Homer's ridiculous claim to be a famous tall basketball player while clearly being two people
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag with perfect Homer confidence. The absurdity of the disguise makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Sorry, I had a really big dinner. Whoo! Dinner! But I'm sure one more french fry wouldn't hurt.
jokes.setup: Tom Savini belching during his presentation
jokes.punchline: He tries to explain it away but then makes it worse by continuing to eat
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor but not particularly clever or memorable. Functions as setup for later gag.

jokes.text: Oh, please. I saw Paul Lynde do that same hackneyed trick on Bewitched.
jokes.setup: Tom Savini performing special effects trick
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy dismisses professional FX with obscure pop culture reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy snobbery with specific pop culture knowledge. Character-driven humor that shows his personality.

jokes.text: To the gag, I will give a D-plus. As for the workmanship on the hand, it- Oh. [Groaning]
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy critiquing Tom Savini's trick hand
jokes.punchline: His pedantic review is interrupted when the hand activates and hurts him
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment where Comic Book Guy's arrogance gets comeuppance. Well-structured interruption gag.

jokes.text: Stop your laughing! You're all banned! Banned I tell you!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy being laughed at after getting hurt
jokes.punchline: He responds by banning everyone from his store in his humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Comic Book Guy's petty authority. Character-appropriate overreaction to embarrassment.

jokes.text: Can't go on describing symptoms much longer. [Groans]
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy having heart attack symptoms
jokes.punchline: Even during medical emergency, he's self-aware about his verbose describing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that's perfectly in character. Comic Book Guy remains pedantically self-aware even during a heart attack.

jokes.text: Can't go on describing symptoms much longer.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy having apparent heart attack
jokes.punchline: Self-aware about his own exposition while dying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - character aware of exposition conventions even while dying

jokes.text: Young man, you've had what we call a 'cardiac episode.' Worst episode ever.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining Comic Book Guy's heart attack as a 'cardiac episode'
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy responds with his catchphrase, treating medical episode like TV episode
jokes.explanation: Perfect pun that works on multiple levels. Brilliant use of character's signature phrase in medical context. Near-perfect joke construction.

jokes.text: I'm just kidding. But you would be dead.
jokes.setup: Doctor joking about wearing Comic Book Guy's watch if he died
jokes.punchline: Casual confirmation that Comic Book Guy would actually be dead
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. The casual tone makes the morbid content funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, dear Lord! We call that profession 'the widow-maker!' Or we would if any of the proprietors were married.
jokes.setup: Doctor shocked at Comic Book Guy running comic store
jokes.punchline: Calls it widow-maker, then adds that comic store owners don't get married anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent double-punchline structure. First the dramatic 'widow-maker,' then the deflating reality about comic store owners.

jokes.text: Well, the Super Friends. Well, you should get some friends who aren't printed on paper. What? You mean action figures?
jokes.setup: Doctor asking about friends, Comic Book Guy mentions Super Friends
jokes.punchline: When told to get non-paper friends, he thinks of action figures instead of real people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy obliviousness. Great misdirection showing he still doesn't understand the concept of real friends.

jokes.text: Two 10-year-olds running my store? I mean-Wait. What is this? Bizarro World?
jokes.setup: Bart and Milhouse offering to run his store
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy references Superman's Bizarro World to describe the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific reference. Comic Book Guy naturally frames real-world absurdity in comic book terms.

jokes.text: A carton of malted milk balls, one box confectioner's sugar, a can of chocolate frosting- That's just his shopping list. No. It's his instructions.
jokes.setup: Bart reading Comic Book Guy's supposed store instructions
jokes.punchline: Instructions are actually just his junk food shopping list
jokes.explanation: Good character insight into Comic Book Guy's priorities and lifestyle. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: Uh-uh. You gotta be 40 inches tall for the adult section. Please? Okay. But get on your tippie toes.
jokes.setup: Kid trying to access adult comic section
jokes.punchline: Bart's arbitrary height requirement and then compromise with tippie toes
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic. Bart creates arbitrary rules then immediately compromises, showing kid-like authority.

jokes.text: Mark down the Poochie crap. Then unstick all the Supergirls.
jokes.setup: Bart giving Milhouse work instructions
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to 'Poochie crap' and the practical problem of sticky Supergirl comics
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Poochie and realistic comic store problem. Shows Bart learning the business.

jokes.text: Get out and take your Sacajawea dollars with ya. I'll give you till three. One- [Body Thuds]
jokes.setup: Moe threatening someone about Sacajawea dollars
jokes.punchline: He starts counting but immediately goes to violence at 'one'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe violence - starts traditional countdown but immediately escalates. Good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Hey, Homer, who's the manatee?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy entering Moe's tavern
jokes.punchline: Moe insults Comic Book Guy by comparing him to a manatee
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe insult. Character-appropriate rudeness but fairly standard fat joke.

jokes.text: Very well. I will have a shot of cranberry schnapps. [Chuckles] Uh, these, they're just painted on there.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy ordering sophisticated drink
jokes.punchline: Moe reveals the fancy bottles are fake - just painted decorations
jokes.explanation: Good class clash humor. Comic Book Guy's pretentious order meets Moe's dive bar reality.

jokes.text: Beer is the nectar of the nitwit. Hey, you knockin' beer? Nobody bad-mouths Duff! Ah, a piece a crap.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy insulting beer
jokes.punchline: Bar patrons defend beer, then Comic Book Guy doubles down with 'piece a crap'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating conflict. Comic Book Guy's pretentiousness vs. working-class bar culture.

jokes.text: Oh, now he's ragging on air conditioners. Hey, they keep us cool in the summer, pal.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy mentioning air conditioners dismissively
jokes.punchline: Bar patron takes this as an attack on air conditioners themselves
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation. The barfly's literal interpretation and defense of air conditioners is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: If I wanted to hear mindless droning, I'd befriend an air conditioner.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy dismisses bar conversation
jokes.punchline: Compares humans unfavorably to appliances
jokes.explanation: Great misanthropic observation delivered with Comic Book Guy's characteristic condescension

jokes.text: Is there a word in Klingon for 'loneliness.' Ah, yes. Garl dack!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy asking about Klingon word for loneliness
jokes.punchline: He knows it immediately, revealing his deep loneliness and nerd knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. The fact he knows this word immediately reveals both his nerd credentials and deep loneliness.

jokes.text: Tough break, toots. I need a man who answers to no one. A full manager. Hotchie-machie.
jokes.setup: Milhouse saying he's co-manager
jokes.punchline: Salesman's sexist dismissal and weird 'hotchie-machie' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good sleazy salesman character work. 'Hotchie-machie' is amusingly random catchphrase.

jokes.text: A superhero with glasses! Oh, yeah. Thick glasses. Kinda like yours.
jokes.setup: Salesman showing Biclops comic
jokes.punchline: He notes the glasses similarity to manipulate Bart into buying more
jokes.explanation: Good manipulation tactic by sleazy salesman. Shows how Bart gets taken advantage of.

jokes.text: So how many you need- 500, 600? 600 sounds good. Aw, that's too bad. There's a price break at a thousand. Oh, man! I'll take 2,000.
jokes.setup: Salesman suggesting quantities
jokes.punchline: Each time Bart agrees, salesman reveals a better deal, escalating to absurd 2,000
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating sales manipulation. Classic high-pressure tactics that lead Bart to disastrous overcommitment.

jokes.text: Well, pardon me, Oldie Hawn. Why, you ill-mannered sack of crap! Oh, goody. Now I know whatever happened to Baby Jane.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy bumping into Agnes
jokes.punchline: Exchange of movie-reference insults: 'Oldie Hawn' and 'Baby Jane' reference
jokes.explanation: Clever movie reference battle. Both characters use pop culture knowledge as weapons, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh, goody. Now I know whatever happened to Baby Jane.
jokes.setup: Agnes insults him back
jokes.punchline: References classic horror film about aging actresses
jokes.explanation: Typical Comic Book Guy obscure cultural reference as insult

jokes.text: Biclops? Who's his girlfriend, Lois Lame?
jokes.setup: Hearing about Biclops superhero
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy creates parody name 'Lois Lame' based on Lois Lane
jokes.explanation: Good pun showing Comic Book Guy's expertise and disdain. Quick, character-appropriate joke construction.

jokes.text: How could you spend all our money on a comic book published by LensCrafters?
jokes.setup: Milhouse discovering Bart bought terrible comics
jokes.punchline: Reveals the comic is published by an eyeglasses company, not a real publisher
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal of how bad Bart's purchase was. LensCrafters publishing comics is perfectly absurd business synergy.

jokes.text: Birds won't even use them in their nests.
jokes.setup: Complaining about worthless Biclops comics
jokes.punchline: Even birds, who use anything for nests, reject these comics
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how worthless the comics are. Birds as the ultimate low standard is clever.

jokes.text: Oh, look. You're getting cranky. You haven't had your juice. Well, my straw broke off in the carton.
jokes.setup: Bart treating Milhouse condescendingly like a child
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's pathetic response about his broken straw
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart-Milhouse dynamic. Bart's condescension and Milhouse's pathetic real problem with the straw.

jokes.text: I have feelings! I'm a human boy, just like you! It's a vacuum cleaner, Milhouse. Whatever!
jokes.setup: Milhouse demanding respect, mentioning corn popper
jokes.punchline: Bart corrects that it's a vacuum cleaner; Milhouse doesn't care about accuracy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Even in his moment of assertion, Milhouse gets details wrong and doesn't care.

jokes.text: Alien Autopsy. Illegal Alien Autopsy. Godfather III: Good Version.
jokes.setup: Boys discovering Comic Book Guy's bootleg video collection
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurdity: regular bootleg, 'illegal' version, and impossible 'good' Godfather III
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity. The 'Good Version' of Godfather III is perfect impossible fantasy for comic book guy to have.

jokes.text: Good Lord, Mother! I can see your... figure. Oh, you see a lot more when you do my daily mole check.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy embarrassed by his mother's revealing outfit
jokes.punchline: Agnes escalates with disturbing TMI about daily mole checks
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor escalation. Agnes makes an already uncomfortable situation much worse with TMI.

jokes.text: Well, in that case, my neighbor Homer released a radioactive ape in my house. It's taken over the whole top floor. It wasn't Dad's fault. The ape tricked him.
jokes.setup: Police informant tape about Homer
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about radioactive ape, then Lisa's matter-of-fact defense that the ape tricked Homer
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor. Lisa's casual defense that 'the ape tricked him' treats the ridiculous scenario as completely normal.

jokes.text: [Drunkenly] Well, what do you mean I can't take off my sweater? I'm hot!
jokes.setup: Bootleg tape of children's show host
jokes.punchline: Mister Rogers drunk and aggressive about his sweater
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of wholesome children's TV icon. The sweater detail makes it specifically Mister Rogers.

jokes.text: Her sneer just lights up my day. And we're always finishing each other's insults.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy describing his relationship with Agnes
jokes.punchline: Romantic language applied to hostile relationship - 'finishing each other's insults'
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody of romantic clichés. 'Finishing each other's insults' perfectly captures their toxic dynamic as romantic.

jokes.text: Sunsets. Thank God there's only one of these a day. Could it be any more orange?
jokes.setup: Romantic sunset moment
jokes.punchline: Agnes complains about the sunset's frequency and color
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes character work. She finds ways to complain about universally beautiful things like sunsets.

jokes.text: Springfield will be bombed at will by all friendly nations to calibrate their missiles. [All] Yea!
jokes.setup: Secret government tape about Springfield's fate in nuclear war
jokes.punchline: Town will be bombed by friendly nations for target practice; crowd cheers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about Springfield's expendability. The crowd's enthusiastic 'Yea!' makes it perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Now for total security, I will terminate the cameraman. - [Groans] Thanks a lot, Steve!
jokes.setup: Secret tape narrator mentions killing cameraman
jokes.punchline: Cameraman sarcastically responds before being killed
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with cameraman breaking fourth wall before death

jokes.text: No, sir. We were just exhibiting them for profit without permission.
jokes.setup: Police asking if bootleg tapes belong to boys
jokes.punchline: Bart's technically honest but still incriminating answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - technically truthful while admitting to different crime. Shows his clever but flawed reasoning.

jokes.text: But the owner is in more hot water than- A Japanese tea bag? Why don't you lay off the Asians, Lou?
jokes.setup: Wiggum making culturally insensitive comparison
jokes.punchline: Lou calls out the pattern of Asian references
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware comedy about the repeated Asian references. Lou serves as voice of reason.

jokes.text: Nobody'll buy those. Your store smells. Now kiss me, funny face.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy suggesting romance comics
jokes.punchline: Agnes insults the idea, insults his store, then demands romantic affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes - chains insults with romantic demand. The juxtaposition of 'your store smells' and 'kiss me' is brilliant.

jokes.text: But we'll reduce the sentence if you put your pants on... fast!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy arrested while undressed with Agnes
jokes.punchline: Police urgency about public decency
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe humor about public indecency

jokes.text: Will you wait for me? Are you crazy? My bones are half dust.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy asking Agnes to wait while he's in jail
jokes.punchline: Agnes rejects him citing her advanced age and mortality
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor about aging and mortality. Agnes's blunt honesty about her physical condition is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Look, if you want me to turn, just point. Ow! It's one way! Ow! Now what was that for? Ow! Ow! Ow!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to communicate with radioactive ape
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempts at reasonable communication met with continued violence
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the radioactive ape story. Homer's logical approach to ape communication is classic character work.

tmdb_overview: Bart and Milhouse are banned from The Android Dungeon after stopping Comic Book Guy from buying a box of priceless Star Wars memorabilia for $5, but are hired as his replacements when Comic Book Guy has a heart attack and is advised to leave his job in order to make friends.
